title: Website on iPad has menu blocked by App Bar Suggestion
excerpt: When I try to use monday.com on my iPad, there is a bar across the top of the webpage that continuously shows suggesting to open the Monday.com App. I feel most situations like this have an “x” available to clear the not…

posts.author: saylorvillechurch (Douglas Porter)
posts.cookedHtml:
When I try to use monday.com on my iPad, there is a bar across the top of the webpage that continuously shows suggesting to open the Monday.com App. I feel most situations like this have an “x” available to clear the notification. The bar keeps me from closing windows that open like the discussion or files window.

IMG_86222732×765 193 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Douglas,

Thanks for your patience on this one.

I’ve chatted with our team internally who confirmed that this is a bit of a tricky one… Essentially, iOS recognises that the URL in the web browser is supported by the native app, i.e the native app knows how to open this link. Whenever it happens, iOS will display this banner. At this stage, we are unable to change this behaviour, however the team are aware of the inconvenience. We apologise for the inconvenience

posts.author: saylorvillechurch (Douglas Porter)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hmmmm, I know that many apps do this but usually there is an “x” to remove the banner or they scroll out of the way. See attached photo & video examples:

IMG_0827.jpeg1290×2796 442 KB

(Attachment RPReplay_Final1708434625.mp4 is missing)

posts.author: kgarcia-universalair (Kenneth)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am having the same issue as I work around the features not available on the mobile/app. As it is, the desktop version doesn’t work well on iPad but when we open pulse items to look at updates, I cannot close the item since the “X” is behind the native iOS banner.

It would be great for Monday to keep this in mind, maybe provide a secondary way to close the item window to get back to the board.
